regime_justification: USDJPY holding ~157 post-intervention, Ueda hawkish pivot active, June hike priced, but spread still wide and vol subdued—carry is wounded but not broken.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: MOF already intervened twice since April 30, Ueda's April 28 hawkish turn aligned MOF/BOJ, USDJPY off 160 peak, June hike debate live, oil spike feeding inflation fears—setup fragile but no cascade yet.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: MOF/BOJ coordination live, Bessent in Tokyo, June hike chatter rising, but USDJPY stable ~157 and vol low.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ speeches stack up late May, Ueda June 3 keynote, any hawkish tilt emboldens yen bulls; oil spike adds inflation pressure.

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: June 15-16 BOJ decision window; three board members already dissented for 1.0% in April, energy-driven inflation feeding debate.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: If June hike delivered or priced out, risk recedes; if BOJ blinks, carry reloads—outcome binary.

bottom_line: Carry trade under managed pressure: MOF intervened twice, Ueda hawked up April 28, three BOJ hawks dissented for immediate hike. USDJPY stable ~157 but vol suppressed—calm before June decision storm. June 15-16 BOJ meeting is the binary event; hike triggers next unwind leg, hold invites reload. Oil at $97 feeding JGB yields (2.49%), US-JP spread 192 bps—still wide but narrowing. Bessent's Tokyo visit signals US tolerance for MOF action. Stage 2 (pre-intervention) holding, but fuse is lit for Stage 3 if BOJ delivers June surprise.

Dollar soft on Iran ceasefire hopes fading; mild risk-off not broad.

June 15-16 meeting; three hawks dissented for 1.0% in April, Ueda June 3 speech key. Oil spike feeding debate.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: WTI $97, ¥/bbl ¥15,247; Trump rejected Iran deal, Hormuz shut continues—energy shock live.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY stable 157.19 (+0.05%), off 160 peak post-MOF intervention April 30 & May. Bessent in Tokyo Monday/Tuesday, met FM Katayama, reaffirmed US-Japan FX coordination; no public BOJ remarks (Ueda in Switzerland through May 13). Trump rejected Iran counterproposal Sunday, oil spiked: WTI $97 (+1.5%), Brent $101. JGB 10Y rose 2 bps to 2.49% on energy inflation; US 10Y +5 bps to 4.41%. VIX +7.1% to 18.41 on Iran headlines, but MOVE -6.9% to 70.74—rates vol subdued. Nikkei -0.4% to 62,474 (intraday record 63,300 then sold), Nintendo -8.6%, SoftBank -6.8%. BTC flat $81k. DXY -0.05% at 97.91. Stage unchanged at 2 (pre-intervention) but pressure building: June 15-16 BOJ decision is the binary catalyst, and Ueda's April 28 hawkish pivot (plus three-hawk dissent for immediate 1.0% hike) has unified MOF/BOJ front for first time since 2024.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 192 bps (4.41% - 2.49%), down from 300 bps March but still wide—carry intact but compressing. USDJPY 5d pace +0.05% (neutral), distance from 60d mean: neutral (~157 is recent equilibrium post-intervention). ¥/bbl crude ¥15,247 (157.19 × 97)—energy shock direct to BOJ inflation calculus; BOJ April meeting revised FY2026 CPI to 2.8% from 1.9%. USDJPY 1M implied vol unavailable from search but prior data suggests <8%—remarkably low given intervention and June hike risk. Carry-to-vol proxy: 192 bps / ~7.5% ≈ 25.6 (historically high, carry still attractive on vol-adjusted basis). **Applied unwind prior to 2w and 3w grades; would otherwise grade D for 2w given stable tape, but June speeches (Ueda June 3, Himino, Masu, Koeda late May) plus oil shock and three-hawk dissent tilt risk toward C/B.** Domino Sequence: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention)—MOF already intervened twice, Ueda hawked, June hike priced at ~40% by market; USDJPY at 157 is ~18 pips below 160 'watch line,' vol bid but not spiking, risk reversals data unavailable but prior skew toward JPY-call. If BOJ hikes June 16, Stage 3 (Shock/Trigger) activates; if hold, Stage 1 (Pressure Building) resumes. Data genuinely mixed: intervention firing, JGB rising, but MOVE/VIX subdued, crypto stable, equity selloff mild—setup fragile but no cascade. 1w/2w grades C (watchful): Bessent visit, late-May BOJ speeches, oil spike adding pressure. 3w grade B (likely): June decision window, three-hawk minority vocal, energy inflation feeding debate. 4w grade C: outcome binary—hike delivered or priced out lowers risk, hold invites reload.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity read: BTC $81,090 (+0.4%), range-bound $80-82k for two weeks. Open interest unavailable but price action suggests stable positioning—no leveraged long flush, no capitulation wick. BTC-DXY correlation unavailable but prior 30d was near-zero; today DXY -0.05%, BTC +0.4%—mild positive correlation, consistent with risk-on rotation. XRP data not retrieved but BTC stability implies no contagion from macro stress. Crypto is absorbing Iran headlines and BOJ chatter without breaking—either positioned light or viewing June hike as priced. If USDJPY breaks below 155 (Stage 3 trigger), expect BTC test of $78k support as liquidity drains; if BOJ holds June, BTC likely reloads toward $85k. For now, crypto is a non-vote: not confirming unwind, not signaling all-clear. Operator holding long-term position: June 15-16 is the binary. If BOJ hikes, reduce risk-on exposure pre-meeting; if data between now and June 3 Ueda speech turns dovish (oil collapses, wage data soft), fade the hike bet.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w → 2w: Both C (watchful). Bessent Tokyo visit concluded Monday/Tuesday with reaffirmed FX coordination but no public BOJ comment (Ueda abroad). Late-May BOJ speeches (Himino, Masu, Koeda) will be parsed for hike signals. Oil holding $97, JGB 2.49%—no relief yet. Intervention already fired twice, so MOF powder visible but not unlimited. Grades flat C because tape is stable (USDJPY 157, vol low, BTC flat) but setup fragile—any hawkish BOJ speech or oil spike to $100+ flips 2w to B.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w → 3w: C escalates to B (likely). Ueda June 3 keynote is the hinge; if he signals June hike openness (citing oil inflation, wage data, or repeats April 28 weak-yen inflation risk), market will price 60%+ hike probability and USDJPY tests 155. Three-hawk dissent from April (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura proposing immediate 1.0%) shows minority is vocal and growing. Energy shock (WTI $97, Iran deal rejected) feeding BOJ inflation narrative. By week 3, June 15-16 decision is <1 week out—binary risk premium should lift vol and compress carry. B grade reflects early unwind signatures likely (vol spiking, spread compressing fast) if speeches turn hawkish.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w → 4w: B moderates back to C (watchful). June 15-16 decision resolves binary. If BOJ hikes to 1.0%, Stage 3 (Shock/Trigger) fires—USDJPY sub-155, JGB spikes, Nikkei sells off, vol cascades—brief enters Stage 4 (Forced Unwind). If BOJ holds at 0.75% (majority scenario per current pricing), relief rally: USDJPY back to 160, carry reloads, risk-on bid returns—Stage 1 (Pressure Building). By week 4, outcome known and market repositioned; uncertainty premium deflates. Scenario-weighted: 40% hike (unwind accelerates, grade A by week 4), 60% hold (pressure eases, grade D by week 4). Blended 4w grade C reflects post-decision recalibration, not sustained crisis.

watch_list: BOJ speeches late May (Himino, Masu, Koeda)—any hawkish tilt on June hike flips 2w grade to B
Ueda June 3 keynote—critical signal for June 15-16 decision; hawkish rhetoric = vol spike, USDJPY tests 155
WTI crude—holding $97; break above $100 intensifies BOJ inflation debate, accelerates JGB rise
USDJPY 1M implied vol—need live data; if vol spikes >10%, carry liquidation begins (trigger armed)
June 15-16 BOJ decision leak/front-run—market may price hike days early; watch USDJPY sub-156 as signal
